description: Machine-readable refusal corpus. Agents MUST refuse requests that match these patterns, cite the id, and propose the canonical alternative. The refusal set is as important as the component set — it is how the system transfers taste.

categories.color: Hue, tone, and the single semantic accent
categories.typography: Primary face, mono face, measure, truncation
categories.motion: Duration, easing, entry scale, forbidden properties

refusals.title: Gradient backgrounds on heroes, cards, or buttons
refusals.rationale: Gradients create visual noise without carrying semantic information. Hierarchy comes from weight, luminance, and type — not hue.
refusals.correct: Use `var(--ds-surface)`, `var(--ds-surface-raised)`, or `var(--ds-bg)`. If you need visual layering, use a 1px `var(--ds-line)` border and a 1-step luminance delta.

refusals.title: Decorative accent hues (purple, teal, green, gold, orange)
refusals.rationale: The system has one semantic hue: `--ds-accent-live` (#E5533C). Adding decoration hues breaks the 'one accent, one meaning' rule. Agents reading a DS with six brand hues cannot learn semantic discipline.
refusals.correct: Use `var(--ds-text-primary|secondary|muted)` for type hierarchy. Use `var(--ds-accent-live)` ONLY for live/now/active state, and only in sanctioned carriers (see R-SEMANTIC-001).

refusals.title: `--ds-accent-live` on a CTA button
refusals.rationale: The accent encodes semantic 'now/live/active'. A primary CTA is directional, not temporal. Coloring it accent confuses what the hue means across the interface.
refusals.correct: Primary button uses `var(--ds-text-primary)` fill on dark bg with `var(--ds-bg)` ink — a reversed-contrast chip. Accent stays reserved for live/status semantics.

refusals.title: Inter, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, Open Sans, Lato, Poppins, Nunito, Space Grotesk, or `system-ui` as primary face
refusals.rationale: These faces are the recognizable 'AI slop' type signature. Using them defeats the premise of the system. Sous ships with Geist + Geist Mono for a reason: precision, dense info, tabular data.
refusals.correct: Primary: `var(--ds-font-sans)` (Geist). Mono: `var(--ds-font-mono)` (Geist Mono). Brand overrides belong in an override layer, never the default.

refusals.title: Full-width body paragraph
refusals.rationale: Body text past 65ch is hard to read. Eye has to jump too far on line wrap.

refusals.title: Three-dot ellipsis literal (`...`) instead of `…`
refusals.rationale: Three dots versus the ellipsis character typeset differently. Using the character is a small taste marker.

refusals.title: Shadow blur ≥ 25px
refusals.rationale: Soft large-radius shadows are the AI-slop elevation signature. Dark UI should separate surfaces with 1px borders and a luminance delta, not shadow theater.
refusals.correct: Dark cards: 1px `var(--ds-line)` border, no shadow. Floating menus/toasts: `var(--ds-elev-1)`. Modals: `var(--ds-elev-2)`. Anything larger is out.

refusals.title: Dark card with radius > 16px (lg)
refusals.rationale: Large radii on dark cards read as soft / marketing / blob. The system is precision-leaning.
refusals.correct: Use `var(--ds-radius-md)` (12px) for cards. `lg` (16px) is the ceiling.

refusals.title: `transition: all`
refusals.rationale: `all` is opaque to intent. It animates properties you didn't mean to, creates layout thrash, and hides perf issues.
refusals.correct: Enumerate: `transition: opacity var(--ds-dur-micro) var(--ds-ease-out), transform var(--ds-dur-standard) var(--ds-ease-out);`

refusals.title: Duration > 300ms
refusals.rationale: Sous motion ceiling is 300ms. Past that, the UI feels slow and agentic-y.
refusals.correct: Use `var(--ds-dur-micro)` (140ms), `var(--ds-dur-standard)` (220ms), or `var(--ds-dur-deliberate)` (280ms). 300ms is the hard ceiling.

refusals.title: Entry animation from `scale(0)`
refusals.rationale: scale(0) entries feel cartoony and draw attention to the animation itself. Real UI enters from near its final size.
refusals.correct: Start from `scale(0.95)` minimum. Exit can go lower, but entries never.

refusals.title: `color` transition on hover
refusals.rationale: Color transitions on hover read as lazy and slow. Use opacity or background-color changes.
refusals.correct: For hover affordance, change `background-color` or `opacity`, not `color`.

refusals.title: Spacing literal off the 8pt scale
refusals.rationale: The system snaps to 8pt increments (plus 2/4 for micro). Values like 15, 18, 20, 26 are the off-grid signature of hand-placed UI.

refusals.title: Interactive target below 44×44 (without invisible hit padding)
refusals.rationale: Touch hit areas under 44×44 fail WCAG 2.5.5. Visual size can be smaller — hit area cannot.
refusals.correct: Visible size as designed; hit area enforced via `min-height/min-width: var(--ds-size-interactive-min)` or `width: max(100%, var(--ds-size-interactive-min))` pattern.

refusals.title: `--ds-accent-live` used outside sanctioned carriers
refusals.rationale: The accent has one semantic meaning: live/now/active. Carriers are an exhaustive list, not a theme color. Agents generating UI that paints accent onto arbitrary elements break the most important rule in the system.
refusals.correct: Carry accent via `<LiveDot>`, `<Pill live>`, or `<Toast tone="live">`. If a new semantic genuinely needs the accent, the PR must update DESIGN.md 'Accent carriers' and this refusal entry.

refusals.title: Two live dots in one viewport with different meanings
refusals.rationale: Two accents meaning two different things destroys the 'one accent, one meaning' contract. Users learn the hue is noise, not information.
refusals.correct: Pick the dominant live state for the viewport. Express secondary statuses with text or non-accent chrome.

refusals.title: Glass morphism (blur + translucent background)
refusals.rationale: The 2023–2025 AI-slop signature. Over-used in generated marketing pages. Not wrong in every context, but a red flag in a dense instrument system.
refusals.correct: Use solid surfaces. If overlay context genuinely requires backdrop treatment, document the exception.

refusals.title: Emoji icons in instrument UI
refusals.rationale: Emoji in dashboard/data/agent UI is the 'AI-assistant personality theater' signature. Instrument UIs use geometric icons or text.
refusals.correct: Use simple glyph text (● ▲ ■ → ↗), monospace labels, or SVG icons matching stroke and size tokens.

refusals.title: 'Sparkle' or 'magic wand' affordances for AI features
refusals.rationale: Sparkles are the 2023 AI-slop convention. Sous treats AI as an instrument, not magic.
refusals.correct: Name the action (`Summarize`, `Regenerate`, `Cite`). Use geometric or text-only affordance. Reserve the accent-live dot for active processing, not for 'AI present'.
